description: This figure presents calculated phase volume fraction diagrams (mole fraction vs. temperature) for two Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels: (a) 9Al-10Mn-0.4C and (b) 9Al-10Mn-1C (in wt%), illustrating thermodynamic gradients governing austenite stability. Both subplots track the equilibrium phase evolution of BCC_A2 (ferrite, α), FCC_A1 (austenite, γ), and KAPPA_E21 (κ-carbide) from 0°C to 1300°C. In (a), BCC_A2 dominates at low temperatures (~0.9 mol fraction) while FCC_A1 remains minimal; a sharp phase transformation occurs around 700°C where BCC_A2 drops precipitously and FCC_A1 rises, reflecting ferrite-to-austenite transformation. In contrast, (b) shows FCC_A1 as the dominant phase at low temperatures (~0.8 mol fraction) for the higher carbon content, with BCC_A2 only appearing above 650°C. The diagrams qualitatively explain the experimental observation that 9Al-10Mn-0.4C undergoes austenite decomposition during tempering at 600°C but not at 800°C, whereas 9Al-10Mn-1C exhibits decomposition at both temperatures due to favorable thermodynamic driving forces indicated by the phase fraction gradients. The κ-carbide phase remains relatively stable (~0.05-0.2 mol fraction) across intermediate temperatures in both alloys.
